title: Varalakshmi Vratam
intro: Varalakshmi Vratam is one of the most important Hindu fasting festivals celebrated in South India, especially in Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, Karnataka, and Tamil Nadu.
description:
Varalakshmi Vratam is one of the most important Hindu fasting festivals celebrated in South India, especially in Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, Karnataka, and Tamil Nadu. The festival is dedicated to Goddess Varalakshmi, a divine form of Goddess Lakshmi who represents wealth, prosperity, happiness, and blessings. Varalakshmi Vratam is observed on the Friday before the full moon day (Shravana Purnima) in the Hindu month of Shravana, usually during July or August.

According to Hindu traditions, Goddess Varalakshmi blesses devotees with prosperity, good fortune, family happiness, and abundance. It is believed that performing this vrat with devotion and following proper rituals brings the blessings of Goddess Lakshmi and removes financial and personal difficulties.

On this auspicious day, women decorate their homes, perform Kalash Sthapana, worship Goddess Varalakshmi, and offer flowers, fruits, sweets, turmeric, kumkum, and traditional offerings. Many married women observe this fast praying for family well-being, marital happiness, the long life of their husbands, and the prosperity of their loved ones. Varalakshmi Vratam symbolizes devotion, faith, gratitude, and divine blessings.

Festival Month

Shravana (July – August)

Significance / Importance

Varalakshmi Vratam holds great religious and spiritual significance in Hinduism. The vrat is performed to seek the blessings of Goddess Varalakshmi, who is believed to bless devotees with wealth, prosperity, good health, happiness, and success.

This festival is especially significant for married women who observe the fast for the well-being of their families, marital harmony, and the prosperity of their homes. Varalakshmi Vratam teaches the importance of devotion, discipline, gratitude, and faith in divine blessings.

Puja Vidhi

1. Wake up early, take a holy bath, and wear clean traditional clothes.
2. Clean and decorate the पूजा area and home.
3. Perform Kalash Sthapana with holy water.
4. Decorate the Kalash with coconut, mango leaves, and sacred items.
5. Place the idol or image of Goddess Varalakshmi.
6. Offer flowers, fruits, sweets, turmeric, kumkum, and Naivedya.
7. Read or listen to the Varalakshmi Vrat Katha.
8. Perform Goddess Lakshmi Aarti and pray for prosperity, happiness, and family well-being.

Related Katha / Mantra / Aarti / Chalisa

* Varalakshmi Vrat Katha
* Lakshmi Mantra
* Shri Sukta
* Lakshmi Aarti
* Lakshmi Chalisa
* Kanakadhara Stotra

faq.a1: Varalakshmi Vratam is observed on the Friday before Shravana Purnima, usually during July or August.
faq.a2: It is celebrated to seek the blessings of Goddess Varalakshmi for wealth, prosperity, happiness, and family well-being.
faq.q3: Where is Varalakshmi Vratam mainly celebrated?
faq.a3: It is mainly celebrated in Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, Karnataka, and Tamil Nadu.
faq.q4: Which deity is worshipped during Varalakshmi Vratam?
faq.a4: Goddess Varalakshmi, a form of Goddess Lakshmi, is worshipped during this vrat.
faq.a5: Many married women observe this vrat for family happiness, marital harmony, and prosperity.
faq.q6: Why is Kalash used in Varalakshmi Puja?
faq.a6: The Kalash represents divine presence, abundance, purity, and the blessings of Goddess Lakshmi.
faq.q7: In which month is Varalakshmi Vratam celebrated?
faq.a7: It is usually celebrated in the month of Shravana (July – August).
faq.q8: What is the spiritual significance of Varalakshmi Vratam?
faq.a8: Varalakshmi Vratam symbolizes devotion, faith, gratitude, prosperity, family happiness, and the divine blessings of Goddess Lakshmi.
